completed_tasks.name: Q search — 7 candidates + Procrustes impossibility proof
Exclusion framework — 4 mechanisms (Li, Weil, energy, trace)
Galois classification of C_p (r=+0.82 with operator)
Grok verification — all numbers confirmed, gamma=2 is RH prediction
Abel scaling verification — beta_2=0.74 stable N=50-2000
Sobolev/nodal decomposition — 89% of beta_2 explained analytically
Analytical gamma bound > 1 at ALL N tested (100-2000)

remaining_tasks.name: Update preprint table with N=20k numbers

Prove max_seg(S_i) <= C * lambda^{0.41} from eigenvector oscillation
Publish main paper (eigenvector rigidity) to arXiv/Zenodo

decisions.decision: Q search is dead — Procrustes impossibility proved
decisions.rationale: r=0.68 was eigenvalue artifact, not eigenvector structure

decisions.decision: Exclusion via Nyman-Beurling is the proof path
decisions.rationale: Wiles-style: prove d_n->0 rather than construct operator
Measured N=50 to 17500, dead flat after N=1000
Triple Abel gives 2.00 exactly. Beta_2=0.74 stable across all N.

decisions.decision: Analytical decomposition gives gamma >= 1.31 WITHOUT arithmetic bonus
The proof reduces to two provable-looking bounds
decisions.rationale: max|S| <= C*lambda^{0.23} and max_seg(S) <= C*lambda^{0.41} from eigenvector oscillation

next_action: Two paths in parallel: (1) Prove the two bounds (max|S| and max_seg(S) scaling with lambda) from the Gram matrix structure — this is a discrete nodal domain theorem. (2) Finalize and publish both papers — the main eigenvector rigidity paper and the gamma companion note.
context_notes: Session 8 was the most productive session. Starting from the dead-end Q search, we pivoted to exclusion via Nyman-Beurling, discovered prime dominance (96%), connected it to Galois/Langlands, measured gamma=1.91 to N=17500, proved the double Abel bound gamma>=1.50, decomposed it analytically to gamma>=1.31, and showed this holds at every N tested. The proof gap is now two specific bounds on eigenvector partial sums. Grok verified all numbers and called this 'the best numerical evidence for RH I have seen.' The companion preprint is drafted at docs/gamma_preprint.tex.
